title: ISP Telemetry: Congestion-vs-Load Law Discovery
strategies: trivial: emit the constant train-mean of y (matches the grader's baseline predictor -> ~0.1)
greedy: OLS on a polynomial basis {1, rho, cv, hop, rho^2}; captures the low-load trend but cannot represent the rho/(1-rho) pole, so it under-predicts the high-load held-out split

strong: OLS on the physically-correct M/G/1 basis {1, hop, q, cv*q} with q=rho/(1-rho); recovers the queueing pole so it extrapolates to the high-load regime, limited only by irreducible held-out noise
invalid: emit a non-parsing / disallowed-token expression -> infeasible (0)
